subject: Gaming
title: China's Game Approval Freeze
description: A 60-second lesson on China's Game Approval Freeze.

hook.text: China froze all new game approvals for nine months in 2018. Thousands of companies were left in limbo.

buildup.text: Chinese regulators restructured the approval process. No new games could legally launch during the freeze.

discovery.text: Tencent lost over $200 billion in market value. Smaller studios faced bankruptcy without new revenue.

twist.text: When approvals resumed, the rules were stricter. Content related to gambling, blood, and certain themes was banned.

climax.text: China's freeze demonstrated that government control can reshape an entire global industry overnight.

punchline.text: Nine months frozen. Two hundred billion lost. Government power reshaped gaming.

quiz.question: How long did China's game approval freeze last in 2018?
